chapter_goal: Make variational principles inspectable through action, first variation, Euler-Lagrange residuals, Legendre nondegeneracy, local minimization, and geodesic energy/length checks.
computational_checks: finite-difference first variation for an endpoint-fixed perturbation
Euler-Lagrange residual for true and perturbed paths
artifact existence and nonzero file-size checks
concept_inventory: action integral as kinetic minus potential energy
integration by parts producing Euler-Lagrange equations
Legendre condition as invertible velocity Hessian
strict convexity in velocity and Wirtinger inequality for local minima
geodesic action, constant speed, and length minimization

source_span_read.boundary_note: Physical page 129 begins Lecture 20; extraction to 132 was used only to see the boundary.

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Follow how endpoint-fixed variations and integration by parts lead to E-L, then how convexity and Wirtinger give a local minimum.
Compare perturbed paths with the true harmonic-oscillator solution and read the action slope at epsilon=0.
visual_sequence.validation: absolute first variation < 1e-5 and positive second variation

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Locate the interval lengths where the second variation is positive.
visual_sequence.validation: positive before pi/omega and negative after pi/omega for the test mode

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Move across interval lengths and see when epsilon=0 changes from a local minimum to a saddle direction.
visual_sequence.validation: HTML file exists and Q(T) sign changes at pi/omega

visual_sequence.inspection_target: On the sphere, compare the great-circle arc with a detour between the same endpoints.
visual_sequence.validation: great-circle length and action are smaller
